title: Constitutional/Civil Rights Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Constitutional/Civil Rights Groups 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Muslim Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advocacy organization concerned with cases involving questions of civil liberties, including rights guaranteed by the US and State Constitutions. Does not provide direct representation to individuals or organizations, but advocates for the passage of laws to protect civil rights and reviews and monitors proposed ballot initiatives. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Visit Website More Details AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION OF OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advocacy organization concerned with cases involving questions of civil liberties, including rights guaranteed by the US and State Constitutions. Does not provide direct representation to individuals or organizations, but advocates for the passage of laws to protect civil rights and reviews and monitors proposed ballot initiatives. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works to enhance understanding of Islam, encourage dialogue, protect civil liberties, empower American Muslims, and build coalitions that promote justice and mutual understanding. Both the national and chapter offices field reports of hate crimes, harassment, and other forms of discrimination related to being Muslim. Issues action alerts to generate grassroots responses to political, social, and media-related issues. Various CAIR publications, including an annual civil rights report, are available online. CAIR and its chapters regularly sponsor voter registration drives across the country to increase Muslim participation in the political arena. Organizes conferences and seminars about Islam and Muslims for government and law enforcement agencies, media professionals, and the academic community. Diversity training workshops are offered for employers, educators, and organizations. 453 New Jersey Avenue SE Washington, DC 20003 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (202) 488-8787 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works to enhance understanding of Islam, encourage dialogue, protect civil liberties, empower American Muslims, and build coalitions that promote justice and mutual understanding. Both the national and chapter offices field reports of hate crimes, harassment, and other forms of discrimination related to being Muslim. Issues action alerts to generate grassroots responses to political, social, and media-related issues. Various CAIR publications, including an annual civil rights report, are available online. CAIR and its chapters regularly sponsor voter registration drives across the country to increase Muslim participation in the political arena. Organizes conferences and seminars about Islam and Muslims for government and law enforcement agencies, media professionals, and the academic community. Diversity training workshops are offered for employers, educators, and organizations. Website Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Enforces laws to ensure everyone has equal access at work, protects against discrimination, and shields workers who report safety problems or use leave. They also protect people who are looking for housing or using public places like stores and transportation. 1800 SW 1st Avenue Suite 500 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 245-3844 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS OREGON OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION
